findings.title: No rate limiting — unbounded LLM cost amplification in dev mode
findings.description: POST /incident has no rate limiting. When API_KEY is blank (dev mode), any caller can trigger 4 sequential Gemini API calls per request with no throttle.
findings.exploit_scenario: Attacker discovers the endpoint is public (no API_KEY set). Sends 1000 POST /incident requests. Each triggers intake+triage+RCA+notify LLM calls. Result: thousands of Gemini API calls, significant API bill, possible quota exhaustion.
findings.impact: Financial: unbounded Gemini API spend. Availability: quota exhaustion blocks legitimate runs.
findings.recommendation: Add slowapi rate limiter: pip install slowapi. 10 req/min per IP on POST /incident is a reasonable starting point.

findings.title: Hardcoded weak secrets in committed docker-compose.yml
findings.description: docker-compose.yml commits NEXTAUTH_SECRET: supersecret, SALT: supersalt, POSTGRES_PASSWORD: postgres to git. These are for local Langfuse dev only but would be dangerous if cargo-culted into a production deploy.
findings.exploit_scenario: Developer copies docker-compose.yml to a staging/prod server and runs docker-compose up without changing secrets. Attacker bruteforces or guesses NextAuth secret, forges session tokens.
findings.impact: Auth bypass on Langfuse observability dashboard if deployed without secret rotation.
findings.recommendation: Add a .env.docker file for compose secrets, reference via env_file:. Add a README warning these values must be rotated before any non-local deployment.

findings.title: GET /incidents/search is fully unauthenticated
findings.description: GET /incidents/search queries Mem0 memory and returns resolved incident data with no authentication. Any browser or script can call it.
findings.exploit_scenario: Attacker calls /incidents/search?q=prod OR /incidents/search?q=password from a browser with no credentials. Gets back historical incident memory potentially including system details, affected services, and root cause summaries.
findings.impact: Information disclosure — internal infrastructure details from resolved incidents exposed to unauthenticated callers.
findings.recommendation: Add Security(verify_api_key) dependency to /incidents/search the same way POST /incident is protected. OR scope it to read-only with a separate read key.

findings.title: Langfuse defaults to http:// — LLM traces sent unencrypted if remote host configured
findings.description: LANGFUSE_HOST defaults to http://localhost:3000. If an operator sets this to a remote Langfuse instance using http:// (not https://), full LLM prompts and responses — including incident details, approver names, and RCA summaries — transit unencrypted.
findings.exploit_scenario: Operator deploys to Azure, sets LANGFUSE_HOST=http://langfuse.internal. Network traffic is intercepted. Attacker reads full LLM traces containing incident details, affected systems, and approval notes.
findings.impact: Sensitive incident data and internal system information exposed in transit.
findings.recommendation: Validate LANGFUSE_HOST starts with https:// at startup when it points to a non-localhost host. Add a warning log if http:// is detected on a remote host.

findings.title: Mem0 retrieval results injected into LLM context without sanitization
findings.description: Mem0 search results are injected verbatim into the triage LLM HumanMessage. If an adversary can store crafted entries in Mem0 (e.g. by triggering a specially crafted incident that resolves and stores memory), those entries influence future triage decisions.
findings.exploit_scenario: Attacker triggers INC-999 with a description crafted to produce a malicious Mem0 memory entry after resolution. Future triage runs for other incidents pull that entry and it skews severity classification or triage notes.
findings.impact: RAG data poisoning — adversarial memory entries manipulate agent outputs for future incidents.
findings.recommendation: Strip or length-limit Mem0 results before injection. Consider a max character cap (e.g. 500 chars per memory entry) and log when results are truncated.
